name: Empire State Building
description: The most iconic skyscraper in the world. 86th floor observation deck has 360-degree open-air views. Go at sunset for magic. Express pass worth it to skip the line. Go between 8-10 AM or after 10 PM to avoid crowds.

name: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island
description: The symbol of America. Take the ferry from Battery Park to Liberty Island, then Ellis Island for the immigration museum. Reserve pedestal access at least 2-3 weeks in advance. First ferry at 8:30 AM avoids crowds.

name: Central Park
description: 843 acres of green in the middle of Manhattan. Bethesda Fountain, Bow Bridge, The Mall, Belvedere Castle, and Strawberry Fields. Enter at 72nd St west side for the best first impression. Rent Citi Bikes for $4.49 per ride.

name: Times Square
description: Love it or hate it, you have to see it once. 15 blocks of neon sensory overload and Broadway theaters. Best at night. See it for 10 minutes, take a photo, then leave. TKTS booth sells same-day Broadway tickets at 20-50% off.

name: Top of the Rock
description: Observation deck at 30 Rock with the best view of the Empire State Building. Three levels including an open-air top deck. Better for photos because you GET the Empire State in your shot. Book sunset tickets ahead.

name: The High Line
description: Elevated park built on a former railway running 1.45 miles through Chelsea and Meatpacking. Art installations, gardens, and incredible architecture views. Enter at Gansevoort St south and walk north. Stop at Chelsea Market for food.
address: The High Line, New York, NY 10011

name: 9/11 Memorial & Museum
description: Two massive reflecting pools where the Twin Towers stood. The underground museum is emotionally powerful and essential. Allow 2-3 hours. Memorial plaza and pools are free. Museum tickets should be booked online ahead of time.

name: MET Museum
description: One of the greatest art museums on Earth with 2 million works spanning 5,000 years. Egyptian Temple of Dendur, European paintings, and seasonal rooftop bar are highlights. Friday and Saturday open until 9 PM and much less crowded.

name: Grand Central Terminal
description: The most beautiful train station in the world. Main concourse ceiling painted with zodiac constellations. The Whispering Gallery lets you whisper into one corner and hear it across the room. Oyster Bar downstairs since 1913. Campbell Bar is hidden inside.

name: DUMBO & Brooklyn Bridge Park
description: The most Instagrammed spot in NYC -- Manhattan Bridge framed by Washington Street cobblestones. Brooklyn Bridge Park has jaw-dropping skyline views, Jane Carousel, and great pizza nearby. Photo spot is between Water and Front Streets on Washington St.

name: One World Observatory
description: 100th floor of the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere. SkyPod elevator shows NYC history as you ascend in 47 seconds. 360-degree views. Less crowded than Empire State or Top of the Rock. ONE Dine restaurant on 101 is surprisingly good.

name: Wall Street & Charging Bull
description: The financial capital of the world. Walk past the NYSE, Federal Hall where Washington was inaugurated, and the iconic Charging Bull. Free to walk around. Trinity Church cemetery has Alexander Hamilton grave.
